Notes: I discussed the risks of opioid medications for pain
management. These risks include addiction, substance abuse,
medication tolerance, physical dependence, respiratory depression,
constipation, nausea, and an opioid-induced hyperalgesic state
(increase in pain from opioid medications). The patient has been
warned to keep their medication in a safe place. Proper use, storage,
and disposal of the medication was discussed. They have also been
told not to drink alcohol while using the medication. The patient has
been informed that The Center for Wellness and Pain Care of Las
Vegas conforms to all of the recommendations of the Nevada
Medical Board. Specific risks were discussed with the patient
including not operating a vehicle while taking opioids because of
increased risk of a motor vehicle accident. We discussed how
